compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Configure a Jira project whose workflow has a status with no entry in the reconciler's `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` map (e.g. a custom state like "In UAT").
2. Move a Jira issue bound to a **closed** local ticket into that unmapped status.
3. Run a reconcile pass with inbound sync enabled.

**Expected:** the unmapped status is skipped (local status left untouched) and the unmapped status is surfaced as a deduped bridge alert.
**Actual (before fix):** `inbound_differ._map_jira_to_local_fields` did `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS.get(status_raw, "open")` — the `"open"` default silently REOPENED the closed local ticket. This class of bug bit once before (ticket 929a). Audit Reliability finding #7 (MEDIUM).

## Why it matters
Reopening closed work corrupts board state, re-enters tickets into ready/next-batch dispatch, and invalidates the meaning of completion attestations — all with no operator signal.

## What / files
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_differ.py`: `_map_jira_to_local_fields` no longer defaults an unmapped status to `"open"` — it OMITS the status field from the mapped dict when the Jira status has no `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` entry and no `rebar-status:` label. `_diff_jira_vs_local` skips the status field when it is absent from the mapped dict, so no status change is emitted (no silent reopen).
- Observability reuses the EXISTING fetcher alert: `fetcher.py` already emits a deduped `fetcher-unmapped-jira-status` bridge alert (built from the same `config.jira_to_local_status` mapping) for any unmapped status seen in a snapshot — so no second, redundant alert is added in the differ (alert at fetch, skip at diff is the correct split).
- `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`: documents the skip + existing-alert behaviour for unmapped inbound statuses.

## Scope
In: inbound_differ.py skip logic, docs. Out: adding a redundant differ-side alert (the fetcher already alerts); changing any existing status mapping; the `idea↔IDEA` round-trip must stay unaffected.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An inbound Jira status with no mapping is skipped (no local status change) — a closed bound local ticket stays closed
- [ ] The unmapped status is surfaced via the existing deduped `fetcher-unmapped-jira-status` bridge alert (no silent handling)
- [ ] Regression test: an unmapped inbound status against a **closed** bound local ticket emits no status change (`"status" not in changed`); mapped statuses (incl. `IDEA→idea`, `Done→closed`) still flow
- [ ] `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` documents the skip + alert behaviour
- [ ] Existing reconciler/bridge suites stay green; `make test` green

file_impact.reason: skip+alert on unmapped status instead of defaulting open

compiled_state.title: Unmapped inbound Jira status defaults to open — can silently reopen closed local tickets
